title: Supreme Court Declares Right to Walk on Safe Footpaths a Fundamental Right
summary: The Supreme Court of India has ruled that the right to walk on footpaths is a fundamental right of citizens. The Court directed that all roads must have footpaths, linking pedestrian safety to the right to life and personal liberty under Article 21. The bench also called for legislation to ensure safe walking infrastructure across the country. This ruling expands the judicial interpretation of fundamental rights to include safe public mobility.
exam_angle: Directly relevant to GS Paper 2 (Polity) — expansion of Article 21 (Right to Life) through judicial interpretation, a recurring UPSC theme. Also tests knowledge of PIL jurisprudence and the Supreme Court's role in enforcing directive principles.
key_facts: Right to walk on footpaths declared a Fundamental Right by Supreme Court
Grounded in Article 21 (Right to Life and Personal Liberty)
Court sought enactment of a law mandating footpaths on all roads
Continues the trend of expansive interpretation of Article 21
source: The Indian Express / India Today / Business Standard

title: India-UK Free Trade Agreement: Absence of Investment Chapter Not a Concern, Says Expert
summary: Amid ongoing discussions on the recently concluded India-UK Free Trade Agreement (FTA), experts including Sir Anton Muscatelli have stated that the lack of an investment chapter in the deal is not a significant concern. The FTA, finalised in May 2025, focuses primarily on goods and services trade liberalisation. India's Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal separately noted that the trade deal cannot be fully implemented until India secures a 'competitive advantage' in key sectors. The deal is seen as a landmark bilateral economic arrangement post-Brexit.
exam_angle: Tests knowledge of India's bilateral trade agreements, FTA structure (goods, services, investment chapters), and India-UK economic relations — relevant for GS Paper 2 (IR) and GS Paper 3 (Economy).
key_facts: India-UK FTA finalised in May 2025
Deal covers goods and services but lacks an investment chapter
Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal flagged need for competitive advantage before implementation
First major trade deal between India and UK post-Brexit

title: US Praises India's GST, Says It Will Boost Bilateral Trade Ties
summary: The United States has expressed a bullish outlook on the Indian economy, specifically praising India's Goods and Services Tax (GST) framework as a trade-facilitating mechanism. US officials stated that GST's unified indirect tax structure reduces trade barriers and will help strengthen India-US economic relations. The remarks come amid ongoing India-US trade negotiations and tariff discussions. This is significant as the US has historically flagged India's tax regime as a trade barrier.
exam_angle: Relevant to GS Paper 3 (Economy) — GST's role in trade facilitation and India-US trade relations. Also relevant for GS Paper 2 (IR) in the context of bilateral economic diplomacy.
key_facts: US praised India's GST as a trade-facilitating tax structure
GST replaced a complex multi-layered indirect tax system in India (implemented July 1, 2017)
Remarks made in the context of ongoing India-US trade negotiations
US had previously cited India's tax policies as trade barriers

title: Iran-US Nuclear Talks: Iranian Negotiators Travel to Switzerland for Peace Discussions
summary: Iranian negotiators departed for Switzerland to hold discussions with the United States on a potential peace or nuclear deal, signalling a renewed diplomatic effort between Tehran and Washington. Separately, Iranian crude oil loading resumed at Kharg Island after a reported US blockade was lifted, easing concerns about oil supply disruptions. Kharg Island handles the vast majority of Iran's crude oil exports. These developments are significant amid global energy market sensitivities.
exam_angle: Tests knowledge of Iran nuclear negotiations (JCPOA context), Kharg Island's strategic importance as Iran's primary crude export terminal, and its impact on global oil supply — relevant for GS Paper 2 (IR) and GS Paper 3 (Economy/Energy).
key_facts: Iran-US talks being held in Switzerland (neutral venue)
Iranian crude loading resumed at Kharg Island after US blockade lifted
Kharg Island handles ~90% of Iran's crude oil exports
Talks are part of broader efforts to resolve nuclear and sanctions disputes

title: NSE Highlights Monsoon, El Niño, and Market Risks as Key Challenges for India's 2026 Economy
summary: The National Stock Exchange (NSE) has identified monsoon variability, El Niño climate patterns, and volatile market trends as key risks for the Indian economy in 2026. The report underscores that agricultural output, which is monsoon-dependent, significantly influences rural demand, inflation, and GDP growth. El Niño events are associated with below-normal rainfall in India, threatening food security and fiscal management. NSE's risk assessment highlights the continued structural dependence of India's economy on agro-climatic conditions.
exam_angle: Links GS Paper 3 topics of Indian economy, agriculture, and climate risks. El Niño's impact on Indian monsoon is also a GS Paper 1 (Geography) topic. Tests understanding of macroeconomic risk factors.
key_facts: NSE identified monsoon, El Niño, and market volatility as top risks for India's 2026 economy
El Niño typically causes below-normal rainfall in India, affecting agriculture
Agricultural sector contributes ~17-18% to India's GDP and employs ~45% of workforce
Report issued by National Stock Exchange (NSE) of India

title: Modi Government's Initiative to Reverse India's Brain Drain
summary: The Government of India under Prime Minister Narendra Modi has launched initiatives aimed at reversing the brain drain phenomenon — the emigration of highly skilled professionals, scientists, and researchers to foreign countries. The efforts include creating better research ecosystems, improving compensation structures in public institutions, and promoting deep-tech and innovation hubs within India. Experts such as Neelkanth Mishra have emphasised the need to invest in deep technology capabilities and build globally competitive products domestically. The initiative aligns with India's broader goal of becoming a global innovation hub.
exam_angle: Relevant to GS Paper 2 (Governance) and GS Paper 3 (Economy/Sci-Tech) — brain drain, human capital development, and India's R&D ecosystem are important exam themes. Also connects to National Education Policy 2020 and Anusandhan NRF.
key_facts: Government initiatives aim to retain skilled talent and attract diaspora back to India
Focus on deep-tech, research ecosystem, and globally competitive product development
Connects to Anusandhan National Research Foundation (NRF) established under NRF Act 2023
India loses significant STEM talent annually to the US, UK, Canada, and Australia

title: Nobel Laureate John Jumper Leaves Google DeepMind for Anthropic
summary: John Jumper, the Nobel Prize-winning scientist known for his pioneering work on AlphaFold (AI-based protein structure prediction), has resigned from Google DeepMind to join Anthropic, an AI safety company. Jumper won the 2024 Nobel Prize in Chemistry alongside Demis Hassabis for developing AlphaFold. His move to Anthropic, which is backed by Amazon and Google, highlights the intense competition for top AI talent globally. Anthropic is known for developing the Claude series of large language models.
exam_angle: Relevant to GS Paper 3 (Sci-Tech) — tests awareness of AI developments, AlphaFold's significance in biosciences, Nobel Prize 2024 winners in Chemistry, and the global AI industry landscape.
key_facts: John Jumper won 2024 Nobel Prize in Chemistry for AlphaFold protein structure prediction
AlphaFold developed at Google DeepMind; co-created with Demis Hassabis
Jumper joined Anthropic — AI safety company known for Claude LLM
Anthropic is backed by Amazon and Google; competes with OpenAI and Google Gemini

title: Karnataka Appoints Kunal Vajani and Omar Hoda as Additional Advocate Generals in Supreme Court
summary: The Karnataka state government has appointed Kunal Vajani and Omar Hoda as Additional Advocate Generals to represent the state in the Supreme Court of India. The Advocate General is the highest law officer of a state, appointed under Article 165 of the Indian Constitution. Additional Advocate Generals assist the Advocate General in legal representation before constitutional courts. The appointment reflects Karnataka's active litigation strategy in the Supreme Court.
exam_angle: Tests knowledge of constitutional provisions — Article 165 (Advocate General of the State) and the role of law officers at the state level. Relevant to GS Paper 2 (Polity/Governance).
key_facts: Kunal Vajani and Omar Hoda appointed as Additional Advocate Generals for Karnataka in Supreme Court
Advocate General of State: Appointed under Article 165 of the Indian Constitution
Advocate General is the chief legal adviser to the state government
Additional Advocate Generals support representation in higher judiciary

title: India's IT Industry Positions Itself for AI Data Annotation and 'Unglamorous' AI Work
summary: India's IT industry is strategically positioning itself to capture AI-related outsourcing work from American companies, including data annotation, AI model training support, and other backend AI services. Industry leaders argue that India's large skilled workforce and cost competitiveness make it ideal for this 'unglamorous' but essential AI infrastructure work. This aligns with India's broader vision of becoming a global hub for AI services and digital economy participation. The development is significant given the rapid growth of the global AI industry.
exam_angle: Relevant to GS Paper 3 (Economy/Sci-Tech) — India's IT sector evolution, AI economy, and digital workforce. Also connects to IndiaAI Mission and India's Semiconductor Mission.
key_facts: India's IT sector targeting AI data annotation and AI model support work from US companies
India's IT industry contributes ~7.5% to GDP and employs ~5.4 million professionals
Government launched IndiaAI Mission with ₹10,371 crore budget in 2024
Data annotation is foundational to training large language models (LLMs)
